statBlock.specialAbilities:
Special Attacks: Improved grab, constrict 2d6+7

Special Qualities: Darkvision 60 ft., immunity to electricity,low-light vision, plant traits, resistance to fire 10

Imported from the d20 SRD; verify attacks and skills in-game. Full rules at d20srd.org.

system.biography:
Shambling mounds, also called shamblers, appear to be heaps of rotting vegetation. They are actually intelligent, carnivorous plants.

A shambler’s brain and sensory organs are located in its upper body.

A shambler’s body has an 8-foot girth and is about 6 feet tall when the creature stands erect. It weighs about 3,800 pounds.

Combat

A shambling mound batters or constricts its opponents with two huge, armlike appendages.

Improved Grab (Ex)

To use this ability, a shambler must hit with both slam attacks. It can then attempt to start a grapple as a free action without provoking an attack of opportunity. If it wins the grapple check, it establishes a hold and can constrict.

Constrict (Ex)

A shambler deals 2d6+7 points of damage with a successful grapple check.

Immunity to Electricity (Ex)

Shamblers take no damage from electricity. Instead, any electricity attack used against a shambler temporarily grants it 1d4 points of Constitution. The shambler loses these points at the rate of 1 per hour.

Skills

Shamblers have a +4 racial bonus on Hide, Listen, and Move Silently checks. *They have a +12 racial bonus on Hide checks when in a swampy or forested area.

system.description:
What it does (SRD): The creature is immune to sleep effects (including magical sleep) unless a specific effect overrides immunity.

ThirdEra: On this item’s Details tab, the immunity is under Capability grants (immunity), not in Mechanical effects.

What it does (SRD): The creature is immune to poison: it does not take ability damage or hit point damage from poison, and it ignores other harmful poison effects unless a specific effect overrides immunity.

ThirdEra: On this item’s Details tab, poison immunity is under Capability grants (immunity), not as save or AC bonuses in Mechanical effects. The character sheet lists it with other immunities and defenses from the creature’s items and features.

What it does (SRD): The creature is immune to paralysis unless a specific effect overrides immunity.

ThirdEra: On this item’s Details tab, the immunity is under Capability grants (immunity), not in Mechanical effects.

What it does (SRD): The creature is immune to stunning unless a specific effect overrides immunity.

ThirdEra: On this item’s Details tab, the immunity is under Capability grants (immunity), not in Mechanical effects.

What it does (SRD): The first 10 points of damage from each fire attack or effect are ignored (same pattern as resist energy for fire). This is not immunity: larger hits still deal leftover damage after subtracting 10.

ThirdEra: On this item’s Details tab, resist fire 10 is under Capability grants (energy resistance), not in Mechanical effects. When the creature takes fire damage, apply this reduction together with any other fire resistance or immunity from its traits.
